Aunque un juego digital puede llegar a s: Although a digital game can pose real risks to users’ physical or mental well-being—leading to addiction, bankruptcy, or even death—here we refer to the risks within the game’s enclosed universe that affect the player’s character or avatar. There is no adventure without risk. Typically a game character has a limited number of lives or a finite amount of energy. Inside the game we encounter countless threats to the character’s integrity, from a horde of zombies to a mineral from the planet Krypton. Risks, like the game rules, establish limits and act as constraints that force the player to plan moves, conserve scarce resources, and find creative ways to avoid hazards. Every adventure involves something to lose. It appeals directly to our survival instinct—the same drive that has produced humanity’s greatest inventions. What risks can we incorporate into our learning adventure to stimulate the player’s creativity?

Cuando estamos motivados por aprender so: When we are motivated to learn about a topic, we pay little attention to superficial aspects of the experience. The information we care about may be on a barely legible photocopy, or the top authority on the subject might deliver their lecture in pajamas. This is the central route of influence — the one that appeals to the educational value of the information. But the user may be unfamiliar with the topic or the presenter. If the central route fails, the user will attend to peripheral cues and judge the content based on aesthetic or emotional considerations. Both routes are used by digital experiences and rarely appear in pure form. How familiar is our audience with the topic or its presenters? What peripheral cues can we use to make our content more persuasive?
Cuando estamos motivados por nuestra rea: When we are motivated by self-actualization we lose track of time, forget our surroundings and engage our whole being. Self-actualization is not a temporary reward or fleeting satisfaction but the meaning that affirms the whole path traveled.
Cuando salimos al encuentro del usuario,: When we meet the user, our experience will be only a small fraction of their journey. Every day we’re bombarded with infinite stimuli competing for attention. The user can join our experience or ignore it entirely. Every digital experience contains visual or aesthetic cues that promise something and predispose the user either for or against it. What elements of the experience could cause rejection or dropout? Which elements predispose the user positively toward the learning experience?

Dentro de un juego las confusas leyes de: Within a game, the confusing laws of ordinary life are temporarily replaced by precise, arbitrary rules freely accepted by the player from the moment the adventure begins. Rules constrain the game to certain boundaries that must not be broken under penalty of fines or infractions. A player will discover the universe’s rules by looking for shortcuts to reach goals or earn rewards. In a learning context, rules delay immediate gratification, stimulate observation, analysis and strategic thinking. What are our game’s rules? How do we use rules to educate and stimulate the player?

Detrás de la usabilidad de un producto o: Behind a product or service's usability lies a disquieting fact: the digital experience has been designed to persuade us — to move us from indifference to adoption and, in many cases, to compulsive use. In this article we will focus on using those techniques for a nobler purpose: creating digital learning experiences that captivate and educate users.
Diariamente, visitamos decenas de sitios: Every day we visit dozens of websites and use apps for an increasing number of routine activities: listening to a favorite song, exercising, learning a recipe, studying or working online. We easily forget that each of these experiences has been designed. In fact, we judge a tool’s usability by its transparency. The tool is there as an extension of our will; we only notice it when our internet connection fails or our device battery dies. But behind a product or service’s usability lies a disquieting fact: the digital experience has been designed to persuade us — to move us from indifference to adoption and, in many cases, to compulsive use.

En especies sociales como la nuestra, el: In social species like ours, play is an opportunity to compete, collaborate, anticipate a co-player’s actions, or simply enjoy a inherently pleasurable activity with others. Video games that allow two or more players to participate simultaneously leverage users’ social psychology. Players compete for rewards or for who reaches the goal first. They seek to influence others’ decisions, becoming allies, opponents, leaders, or specialists. Likewise, a co-player’s actions can be instructive and model behavior for other players, fostering social learning. What elements of competition, collaboration, or social learning opportunities can we include in our digital adventure? How do we integrate our learning solution with social networks?
En este artículo hemos visto todo lo que: In this article we have reviewed what persuasive technologies can offer to create more effective, engaging, and meaningful digital learning experiences. Some instructional content lends itself better to full gamification, while for other content it may be enough that the user simply achieves a learning objective without technology becoming a barrier. The important thing is to know the tools available and the influence they can have on user behavior. Approaching their psychology and sources of motivation will allow us to create learning solutions that meet both their goals and ours.

We want to achieve our goal in the simplest, most direct way possible without effort or annoying interruptions. That’s why UX designers often shape decision processes so that navigating their interfaces feels like driving on a high-speed highway in a car that basically drives itself. La experiencia de usuario, conocida como: User experience, known as UX, is an interdisciplinary field that studies how users interact with a product or service. It seeks to understand users’ behaviors, emotions and underlying attitudes to design experiences that are more intuitive, effective and engaging, thereby increasing adoption. The rise of digitalization and automation, the predominance of mobile devices and broader digital literacy are among the factors behind UX’s boom, but the emerging discipline is rooted in human-computer interaction (HCI) fundamentals and the application of behavioral psychology to technological design. Below we review what UX offers for designing more effective and satisfying learning experiences.
La funcionalidad, la estética y las micr: Functionality, aesthetics and micro-rewards in each interaction create an invisible but constant rhetoric, and before we know it the product or service has become part of our lives. The fundamentals of persuasion are used daily by the decade’s most popular apps — Facebook, Instagram, YouTube and Twitter. Video games, meanwhile, are engaging more consumers with narratives of adventure and mastery; their market already surpasses the music and film industries combined and was projected to generate hundreds of billions of dollars annually by 2023. Like any tool, the design of persuasive technologies has a dystopian flip side: their use can quickly become abuse, with harmful consequences for users’ mental and social well-being. In this article, however, we will focus on their use for a nobler purpose: applying UX and gamification techniques to digital products capable of fulfilling the Ciceronian maxim — to teach, to delight and to move.
La gamificación es el arte de aplicar lo: Gamification is the art of applying the elements that make a game stimulating and addictive to a daily, workplace or educational context. Although gamification is controversial due to its capacity to act directly on dopamine levels, its use in learning contexts can have positive effects on concentration, alertness and motivation. Through mechanics, dynamics and aesthetics borrowed from video games, we can engage users around the pedagogical goal of our digital adventure. Below we review the main game elements we can use as motivators.
la información recuperada, evitando aluc: the retrieved information, avoiding hallucinations and protecting your intellectual property.
La interactividad transforma la presenta: Interactivity turns information presentation into an active experience that promotes more effective, meaningful, and lasting learning.
Las recompensas de una aventura digital: The rewards in a digital adventure can be coins, diamonds, extra lives, or the new status the player acquires. Rewards are a valuable opportunity to add layers of satisfaction to the game experience. A reward is satisfying when it combines visual and auditory effects that delight the senses. These types of rewards exert a powerful reinforcing effect on the player, so in a learning context they can be useful for building habits or developing skills that require sustained practice. However, if rewards depend solely on the player’s skill, they become too predictable and their effect diminishes over time. One reason gambling games are so seductive is precisely the size of the rewards and the unpredictability of obtaining them. By adding a surprise factor, we can increase the player’s alertness. How am I rewarding the player in my learning adventure? What surprise effects can I include to keep alertness at its peak?
Las tecnologías persuasivas influyen sob: Persuasive technologies influence human behavior using reinforcement pathways similar to those B.F. Skinner employed with lab rats in the 1930s.
Los precios están expresados en UF (Unid: Prices are quoted in UF (Unidad de Fomento). Production lead times are estimated business days and begin upon receipt of final approved scripts/contents. Time ranges represent the average user navigation/playback time.
Los principios de UX y gamificación pued: UX and gamification principles can be used to create digital learning experiences that captivate, sustain attention and educate.
Los usuarios se sumergen en una experien: Users approach a digital experience with certain expectations about how to navigate and interact with our graphical interface. These expectations are based on mental models formed from prior experience. Websites we’ve visited, social networks we use daily and videos we’ve watched on YouTube shape our expectations when using a digital solution. A successful UX transforms the unknown into the known; its language is intuitive and experiential. When faced with a new digital interface we quickly recognize where to click, scroll, swipe or hover. An easy-to-use interface is typically one that is easy to recognize. What language does our learning solution speak? Which familiar functionalities can we incorporate to enrich our content?

Por último, al ser un universo en miniat: Finally, as a miniature universe, the culmination of a game is always the player’s self-realization. The hero’s apotheosis occurs when their tasks are completed; when they reap the fruit of all their efforts. For Hercules it meant becoming a god; for the Buddha, attaining enlightenment and liberation for all beings. For most of us, self-actualization means realizing our full potential and becoming the best version of ourselves. Rules, risks, and rewards appeal to our survival instinct; competition to our thirst for social validation. In those cases, what drives us to act is the desire for something we lack and the satisfaction that ends that desire. But self-actualization allows the player to access a space of personal transcendence, making it the most inexhaustible of motivational sources. When motivated by personal realization, we lose track of time, forget our surroundings, and commit our whole being. Self-actualization is not a prize or a temporary satisfaction, but the meaning that validates the entire journey traveled. Of all motivational sources, this may be the hardest to incorporate into a digital learning adventure, since it requires that our content be suitably aligned to speak the language of transcendence. Is it possible to give our content a transcendent meaning?

que se traducen en español como educar,: which translate into English as to teach, to delight and to move. According to Ciceronian rhetoric, when we present an idea we should not only convince our audience with the weight of arguments or demonstrations; we must also delight them with vivid imagination and inflame their passions with eloquence. The art of rhetoric, as systematized by Aristotle, Cicero and Pseudo-Longinus, is not so different from what political campaigns, mass media, mobile apps and influencer marketing use today. Although it is striking that the fundamentals of persuasion remain largely the same two millennia later, what the Mediterranean philosophers could never have foreseen is their application to designing digital experiences that captivate users as systematically as they are invisibly deployed.

Todas las gestas heroicas, desde Homero: All heroic quests from Homer onward have a mission, which may be stated at the adventure’s outset or remain hidden like the meaning of life itself. Ithaca, the Golden Fleece, the Promised Land or nirvana are all variations of a mission that justifies the adventure and gives transcendental meaning to the hero’s actions. The mission not only organizes the player’s actions coherently but becomes a challenge demanding focus and concentration, helping to produce an optimal mental state for learning to occur. What explicit or implicit mission have we given our player? What transcendental meaning does our mission give to the player’s actions?

Un aventura no es un mero episodio de nu: An adventure is not a mere episode of life but an extraordinary experience that interrupts the ordinary flow of events. Its appeal is that it removes everyday constraints and obligations and ventures into the unknown, where difficulties and extraordinary rewards await. A game calls us to adventure with a meaningful story we can participate in. It makes us lose track of time and fully commit to the activity. Story is the central axis around which all other game features revolve. The story does not have to be realistic — it can take place here and now, in another solar system, or in the future. Una aventura digital nos invita al descu: A digital adventure invites us to discovery. Discovery is a form of exploratory play where we uncover the space of possibilities within the game. Our scientific curiosity urges us to explore the sensory space, run small experiments to see the effects of our actions, and infer the rules and exceptions of this miniature world. This exploratory phase is necessary to familiarize ourselves with the game universe and can be highly beneficial for stimulating generative learning. When an educational adventure is based on a scientific model of a well-described phenomenon — such as natural selection or market behavior driven by supply and demand — we can give the user control over the variables that govern this universe, as simulators from MinuteLabs.io do. Which features of my digital adventure invite players to active discovery? How can I turn exploration and experimentation into learning opportunities?
Una experiencia de aprendizaje digital n: A digital learning experience does not have to be the same as an online purchase or installing a new mobile app. We must balance ease of use with the inherent challenge of the learner’s journey.
